entities.name: vikāra
entities.cite: the transformation of milk or of a seed

entities.name: anupurvī-niyama
entities.cite: natural transformation has anupurvi-niyama, fixed serial order

entities.name: pratipattṝ
entities.cite: the sequence in the cognitions of comprehenders

entities.name: buddhi
entities.cite: the sequence in the cognitions of comprehenders fixed

entities.name: niyata krama
entities.cite: a niyata krama, a law-governed ripening

entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: same sounds, same prepared faculty, same staged path

entities.name: process
entities.cite: manifestation is a causal process with the same fixity as any other maturation

axioms.name: anupurvi_niyama
axioms.cite: natural transformation has anupurvi-niyama, fixed serial order

axioms.name: niyata_krama
axioms.cite: the part-seizures of 1.90 are not a random walk that happens to end at the whole but a niyata krama, a law-governed ripening

axioms.name: lawlike
axioms.cite: Error with lawlike trajectory is process, not accident

axioms.name: exemplifies
axioms.cite: Because manifestation is a causal process with the same fixity as any other maturation

denials.name: arbitrary
denials.cite: if the intermediate graspings are errors, why do all competent hearers commit the *same* ones, in the same order, and converge on the same whole?

claims.name: anupurvi_niyama
claims.cite: As there is a fixed order of succession in the transformation of milk or of a seed

claims.name: niyata_krama
claims.cite: so is the sequence in the cognitions of comprehenders fixed

rejected_readings.label: arbitrary_cognitions
rejected_readings.rendering: The sequence of cognitions in comprehenders is arbitrary and varies unpredictably from hearer to hearer.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the verse's assertion of niyata krama (fixed, law-governed sequence), and ignores the evidence that all competent hearers follow the same staged path.

rejected_readings.label: accidental_errors
rejected_readings.rendering: Errors in comprehension are accidental deviations from the correct path, not lawlike stages.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary explicitly states 'Error with lawlike trajectory is process, not accident', insisting that even the errors are integral to the causal process, not random deviations.

rejected_readings.label: formless_transformation
rejected_readings.rendering: Natural transformations like milk becoming cheese have no necessary order of succession.
rejected_readings.why: The verse explicitly grounds cognition's ordered nature in the fixed serial order of natural transformation, which the commentary stresses is governed by anupurvi-niyama, not aniyama (disorder).

notes: The verse defends incremental comprehension by paralleling it with natural causation: both milk-transformation and cognition exemplify niyata krama (law-governed sequence), not randomness. The central ontological move equates understanding with growth-process, subordinating it to general causal fixity.
